title: Gram Matrix
description:
Implement the Gram matrix of $X \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times n}$ (m rows/vectors of dimension n):
$$G = X X^\top \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m}, \qquad G_{i,j} = \sum_{k=1}^{n} X_{i,k} X_{j,k}$$
so entry $G_{i,j}$ is the dot product of row $i$ and row $j$ of $X$.

Implement `gram_matrix(X)` returning `G` of shape `(m, m)`.

**Shapes**: `X`: `(4, 5)` -> returns `(4, 4)`.
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def gram_matrix(X):
    # TODO: X @ X.T
    pass
reference:
import numpy as np

def gram_matrix(X):
    X = np.asarray(X, dtype=np.float64)
    return X @ X.T
hints: The Gram matrix is simply X multiplied by its own transpose: X @ X.T.
Entry (i, j) of the result is the dot product of row i and row j of X, so the diagonal holds each row's squared norm.

solutions.name: Matrix product with transpose
solutions.explanation: Matrix multiplication $XX^\top$ pairs every row of $X$ with every row of $X$ via dot products, producing the $m \times m$ matrix of pairwise inner products, i.e. the Gram matrix; it is symmetric since $G_{i,j} = G_{j,i}$.
solutions.code:
import numpy as np
def gram_matrix(X):
    X = np.asarray(X, dtype=np.float64)
    return X @ X.T
